title: Podman build multi-stage with squash should not remove intermediate base image from local cache
body:
### Issue Description

Building a multi-stage image with squash removes intermediate base image from local cache.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue

`podman build -t parent-one - <<EOF
FROM alpine
RUN echo "parent-one" > parent-one
EOF

podman build -t parent-two - <<EOF
FROM alpine
RUN echo "parent-two" > parent-two
EOF

podman build -t multi-stage - <<EOF
FROM localhost/parent-one as p1
FROM localhost/parent-two
COPY --from=p1 parent-one .
EOF


podman build -t multi-stage-squash --squash - <<EOF
FROM localhost/parent-one as p1
FROM localhost/parent-two
COPY --from=p1 parent-one .
EOF

podman image ls
`

### Describe the results you received

local/parent-one built image vanished from my local cache.
Note, if we remove the '--squash' option, it persists.

![image](https://github.com/containers/podman/assets/5895852/0f38d3f6-441e-4a21-b86f-4ca731450e16)


### Describe the results you expected

The image "parent-one" should have not been removed from the local cache.

Which is the case when I remove the --squash option.
Note, I was not able to reproduce with buildah.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

I'm in VirtualBox, buy tried this also out of VM in redhat 8.8

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: Using `--layers` with `--squash` should prevent this but there is also a bug in buildah and PR https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/5081 should fix it.
@gabs79 You are using very old version of podman newer version allows `--squash-all` and `--layers` together.

> Using `--layers` with `--squash` should prevent this but there is also a bug in buildah and PR [containers/buildah#5081](https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/5081) should fix it.

You are right, I was able to prevent this behavior with a newer version of Podman 4.7.1., by using `--squash-all` and `--layers` together.
Although, I'm assuming with the proper fix above, it would only need --squash, correct?
Yes the fix is in the above PR
